title: The counter that refuses to count

options: JavaScript variables can't be changed inside a function
The button needs an `onSubmit` handler instead of `onClick`
React only re-renders when state is updated through a setter like `setCount`
The variable must be declared with `const` for React to track it
mcq.explanation_md: The variable *does* change in memory — but React has no way of knowing it happened, so it never redraws the screen. React re-renders a component only when you update state through the setter from `useState` (or when props change). That's the whole point of `const [count, setCount] = useState(0)`: calling `setCount` both stores the new value and tells React "something changed, render again." Option A is the tempting trap — the click handler runs fine and `count` really becomes 1; the bug isn't that the variable can't change, it's that changing it silently updates nothing on screen.
